title: hs:shape technical-POC frontmatter
description: Shape of one POC-<n>.md frontmatter block under docs/product/shape/poc/, as written by poc_gate.py. A POC is closed only when both the review verdict and the (optional) verification verdict read back as exactly PASS -- see gate(). poc_gate.py never runs, re-derives, or spawns a review itself; it only reads an already-produced verdict artifact.

id.description: Parent-free, globally monotonic within the workspace POC dir (max existing POC-<n>.md filename + 1, never reused).

plan_id.description: plans/<plan_id> that verifies this POC. Null until gate() is called with a plan_id -- a POC is often authored before its verifying plan exists.

status.description: author() writes 'open'; gate() flips it to 'closed' only when both verdicts read back PASS, otherwise it stays whatever it already was.

verdict.description: The review-decision artifact's verdict as last read by gate(), or null when never gated / the artifact was absent, unreadable, or carried a verdict value outside the known set (see verdict_unknown).

verification_verdict.description: Same shape as verdict, for the paired verification artifact.

closed.description: True only when both verdict and verification_verdict read back exactly PASS. PASS_WITH_RISK is a conscious soft-accept upstream, not a closure license here.

review_decision_path.description: Path to the review-decision artifact gate() last read, or null when never gated.

verification_path.description: Path to the verification artifact gate() last read, or null when never gated (verification is optional).

verdict_unknown.description: True when a gated artifact carried a verdict value outside the harness's known verdict set (read from harness/schemas/artifact-review-decision.json, not hand-copied) -- surfaced rather than silently collapsed into the same bucket as a missing artifact. Only present after a gate() call.

gated_by.description: Actor that ran the last gate() call. Only present after a gate() call.

gated_ts.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last gate() call. Only present after a gate() call.
